summary:
The "Great Lakes Fishery Research Reauthorization Act" (Public Law 119-67) is a federal statute enacted to reauthorize funding for monitoring, assessing, and researching the Great Lakes Basin. The primary purpose of the Act is to extend the authorization of these activities to ensure continued scientific oversight of the Great Lakes fishery resources.

To accomplish this, the Act amends existing law, specifically Section 201(d) of title II of division P of the Further Consolidated Appropriations Act, 2020 (codified at 16 U.S.C. 941h(d)). The sole legislative change made by this Act is to strike the year "2025" from the existing funding authorization and insert "2030." This modification extends the authorization of appropriations for the Great Lakes Basin monitoring, assessment, and research programs for an additional five years, through the year 2030. 

The Act contains no other provisions, exceptions, or conditions. It was approved by Congress and signed into law on December 26, 2025.
